India Elections World Sport Data Health Opinion Science Entertainment Premium Videos Podcast Photos Visual Stories Specials TH Explains eBooks TH Games Newsletter Lit For Life The Huddle Home News India Manipur Kuki-Zo protestors rally in Manipur, demanding justice for slain church leaders, release of hostages Kuki-Zo groups call for re-imposition of President’s Rule in memo to Home Minister; claiming 14 people are still hostages, Kuki Inpi Manipur chief says “continued captivity is an act of terror against a people” Published - May 16, 2026 09:16 pm IST - GUWAHATI The Hindu Bureau READ LATER SEE ALL Remove Members of the Kuki community also staged a protest against the killing of three church leaders in Manipur at Jantar Mantar, in New Delhi on May 16, 2026 | Photo Credit: ANI Thousands of Kuki-Zo people took to the streets in Manipur’s Churachandpur and Kangpokpi districts on Saturday (May 16, 2026), demanding the unconditional release of Kuki hostages and justice for three church leaders killed in an ambush by extremists on May 13. ‘Act of terror’ These demands include a National Investigation Agency probe into the killing of the church leaders, a permanent security guarantee for Kuki-Zo civilians and vulnerable villages, the establishment of special security zones in conflict-prone areas, and an expedited political engagement with Kuki-Zomi extremist groups under the Suspension of Operations pact.
